@shortcuts.list.noShortcut.description: Shown in the read-only Keyboard shortcuts help window in a command's shortcut column when that command has no shortcut assigned.

@shortcuts.list.addedTooltip.description: Tooltip on a shortcut chip in the Keyboard shortcuts help window, marking a shortcut the user added or changed from the default. A short label.

@shortcuts.list.disabledTooltip.description: Tooltip on a struck-through shortcut chip in the Keyboard shortcuts help window, marking a built-in shortcut the user turned off. A short label.

@shortcuts.scope.app.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for app-wide commands (those that work anywhere). A short section title.

@shortcuts.scope.mainWindow.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for commands scoped to the main window. A short section title.

@shortcuts.scope.fileList.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for commands that act on the file list (the list of files in a pane). A short section title.

@shortcuts.scope.briefMode.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for commands specific to Brief view (a compact, multi-column file layout). "Brief" matches the view's name elsewhere in the app.

@shortcuts.scope.fullMode.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for commands specific to Full view (a detailed single-column file layout). "Full" matches the view's name elsewhere in the app.

@shortcuts.scope.volumeChooser.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for commands in the volume chooser (the picker for disks and mounted locations). A short section title.

@shortcuts.scope.network.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for commands in the network browser (finding servers on the network). A short section title.

@shortcuts.scope.shareBrowser.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for commands in the share browser (the shared folders on a network server). A short section title.

@shortcuts.scope.commandPalette.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for commands inside the command palette. "Command palette" matches the name used elsewhere in the app. A short section title.

@shortcuts.scope.aboutWindow.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for commands in the About window. A short section title.

@shortcuts.scope.onboarding.description: Group heading in the Keyboard shortcuts list for commands in onboarding (the guided first-time setup). A short section title.

shortcuts.conflict.reservedByMacOs: {combo} is reserved by macOS ({command}) and won''t reach Cmdr. Pick a different combo.

@shortcuts.conflict.reservedByMacOs.description: Warning banner when the user tries to assign a shortcut that macOS owns. Explains the shortcut can't reach the app. "Cmdr" and "macOS" are names; do not translate them. "combo" is a key combination like Command-H.

placeholders.combo: the key combination the user pressed, shown as glyphs (e.g. ⌘H)
placeholders.command: the name of the macOS command that already uses this combination (e.g. Hide Cmdr)

shortcuts.conflict.fixedKey: {combo} is a fixed key in Cmdr ({command}) and can''t be reassigned. Pick a different combo.

@shortcuts.conflict.fixedKey.description: Warning banner when the user tries to assign a shortcut that's a built-in fixed key (one the app hardcodes and can't rebind). "Cmdr" is the brand name; do not translate it. "combo" is a key combination.

placeholders.combo: the key combination the user pressed, shown as glyphs (e.g. ↑)
placeholders.command: the name of the built-in command that uses this fixed key (e.g. Select previous file)

shortcuts.conflict.systemShortcut: {combo} is usually taken by macOS ({label}), so it may never reach Cmdr. You can free it up in System Settings > Keyboard.

@shortcuts.conflict.systemShortcut.description: Soft warning when the user assigns a shortcut macOS usually uses for a system feature; they can still keep it. "Cmdr" and "macOS" are names; do not translate them. "System Settings > Keyboard" is a macOS settings path; reuse the localized macOS wording for that screen.

placeholders.combo: the key combination the user pressed, shown as glyphs (e.g. ⌘Space)
placeholders.label: the macOS feature that usually uses this combination (e.g. Spotlight)

@shortcuts.system.spotlight.description: Name of the macOS Spotlight search feature, shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Reuse the localized macOS name for Spotlight.

@shortcuts.system.finderSearch.description: The macOS Finder search window, shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. "Finder" is the macOS app name; reuse the localized macOS wording.

@shortcuts.system.characterViewer.description: The macOS Character Viewer (emoji and symbols picker), shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Reuse the localized macOS name.

@shortcuts.system.inputSourceSwitching.description: The macOS feature for switching keyboard input sources (layouts/languages), shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Lowercase because it appears mid-sentence.

@shortcuts.system.appSwitcher.description: The macOS app switcher (Command-Tab), shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Lowercase because it appears mid-sentence after "taken by macOS (".

@shortcuts.system.missionControl.description: The macOS Mission Control feature, shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Reuse the localized macOS name.

@shortcuts.system.appWindows.description: The macOS "App windows" Mission Control feature, shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Reuse the localized macOS name.

@shortcuts.system.spaces.description: The macOS Spaces (virtual desktops) feature, shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Reuse the localized macOS name.

@shortcuts.system.screenshots.description: The macOS screenshot shortcuts, shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Lowercase because it appears mid-sentence.

@shortcuts.system.screenRecording.description: The macOS screen-recording shortcut, shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Lowercase because it appears mid-sentence.

@shortcuts.system.loggingOut.description: The macOS log-out shortcut, shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Lowercase because it appears mid-sentence.

@shortcuts.system.lockingScreen.description: The macOS lock-screen shortcut, shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Lowercase because it appears mid-sentence.

@shortcuts.system.forceQuit.description: The macOS Force Quit dialog, shown inside a shortcut-conflict warning. Reuse the localized macOS wording for Force Quit.

shortcuts.section.title: Keyboard shortcuts

@shortcuts.section.title.description: Title of the Keyboard shortcuts section in the Settings window.

@shortcuts.section.searchByNamePlacehold.description: Placeholder in the input that filters the shortcut list by command (action) name. The trailing "..." suggests typing; keep it.

@shortcuts.section.filterByKeysPlacehold.description: Placeholder in the input that filters the shortcut list by pressing a key combination. The trailing "..." suggests typing; keep it.

@shortcuts.section.pressEscToClear.description: Hint below the key-filter input telling the user how to clear it. "ESC" is the Escape key; keep it as the key name.

@shortcuts.section.filterAll.description: Filter chip in the Keyboard shortcuts section showing every command. A short toggle label.

@shortcuts.section.filterModified.description: Filter chip in the Keyboard shortcuts section showing only commands whose shortcuts the user changed. A short toggle label.

@shortcuts.section.filterConflicts.description: Filter chip in the Keyboard shortcuts section showing only commands with conflicting shortcuts. A count badge may follow it. A short toggle label.

@shortcuts.section.cancel.description: Button that dismisses the shortcut-conflict warning without making a change. A short button label.

@shortcuts.section.useAnyway.description: Button that assigns the shortcut despite a soft warning that macOS usually uses it. A short button label.

@shortcuts.section.keepBoth.description: Button that keeps the new shortcut AND the other command's existing one (both stay bound to the same combination). A short button label.

@shortcuts.section.removeFromOther.description: Button that takes the shortcut away from the command that already had it, freeing it for the new command. A short button label.

@shortcuts.section.alreadyBound.description: Warning shown when the user assigns a shortcut already used by another command. The combo is wrapped in (rendered bold); keep the tags around it. The command name is shown in quotes.

placeholders.combo: the key combination the user pressed, shown as glyphs (e.g. ⌘C)
placeholders.command: the name of the command that already uses this combination (e.g. Copy to clipboard)

@shortcuts.section.modifiedTooltip.description: Tooltip on the dot marking a command whose shortcuts differ from the shipped defaults.

@shortcuts.section.hasConflictsTooltip.description: Tooltip on the warning icon marking a command that shares a shortcut with another command.

@shortcuts.section.noneShortcut.description: Placeholder shown in a command's shortcut column when no shortcut is assigned. The parentheses signal it's a state, not a value; keep them.

shortcuts.section.macOsTooltip: macOS handles this shortcut. Cmdr can''t change it.

@shortcuts.section.macOsTooltip.description: Tooltip on the "macOS" badge of a read-only shortcut row, explaining why it can't be changed. "macOS" and "Cmdr" are names; do not translate them.

shortcuts.section.fixedTooltip: This key is built into Cmdr and can''t be changed.

@shortcuts.section.fixedTooltip.description: Tooltip on the "Fixed" badge of a read-only shortcut row, explaining why it can't be changed. "Cmdr" is the brand name; do not translate it.

@shortcuts.section.macOsBadge.description: Badge on a read-only shortcut row meaning macOS owns this shortcut. "macOS" is a name; do not translate it. A short tag.

@shortcuts.section.fixedBadge.description: Badge on a read-only shortcut row meaning the key is hardcoded in the app and can't be rebound. A short tag.

@shortcuts.section.pressKeys.description: Prompt shown in a shortcut pill while it's waiting for the user to press a key combination. The trailing "..." signals it's waiting; keep it.

@shortcuts.section.removeShortcutTooltip.description: Tooltip on the × button that removes a single shortcut from a command. A short label.

@shortcuts.section.addShortcutTooltip.description: Tooltip and accessibility label on the + button that adds another shortcut to a command. A short label.

@shortcuts.section.resetToDefaultTooltip.description: Tooltip and accessibility label on the button that restores a single command's shortcuts to the shipped defaults. A short label.

@shortcuts.section.resetAll.description: Button at the bottom of the Keyboard shortcuts section that restores every command's shortcuts to the shipped defaults.
